sourceMovie.title: The Mask
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Mask for the same reason it broke through in 1994: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chuck Russell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Weird Science
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mask clicked for you, Weird Science (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shrek
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mask clicked for you, Shrek (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Batman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mask clicked for you, Batman (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Mask?
faq.answer: Try Weird Science, Shrek, Batman—each shares DNA with The Mask while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Mask?
faq.answer: The Mask (1994) is best described as Romance, Comedy, Crime, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Mask part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Mask Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
